revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]Created page with 'Takeda Shrine is the Shinto shrine that houses the diety of Takeda Shingen. [[Image:TakedaShrine.jpg|thumb|Takeda Shrine]]'
revision.text:
Takeda Shrine is the Shinto shrine that houses the diety of Takeda Shingen.
[[Image:TakedaShrine.jpg|thumb|Takeda Shrine]]

'''Takeda Shrine''' is the [[Shinto shrine]] that houses the deity of [[Takeda Shingen]].
[[Image:TakedaShrine.jpg|thumb|Takeda Shrine]]
[[Category:Shinto shrines]]
{{shinto-stub}}

'''Takeda Shrine''' is the [[Shinto shrine]] that houses the deity of [[Takeda Shingen]].
[[Image:TakedaShrine.jpg|thumb|Takeda Shrine]]
[[Category:Shinto shrines]]
{{shinto-stub}}
[[ja:武田神社]]

[[Image:TakedaShrine.jpg|thumb|Takeda Shrine]]
{{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[Japan]], and houses the deity of [[Takeda Shingen]].
[[Category:Shinto shrines]]
{{shinto-stub}}
[[ja:武田神社]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = TakedaShrine.jpg | width = 250 | caption = Takeda Shrine | type = | dedication = | founded = [[1919]] | closed = | founder = | priest = | address = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, [[Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu]]
[[Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi]] | phone = | website = http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ }} {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[Japan]], and houses the deity of [[Takeda Shingen]]. [[Category:Shinto shrines]] {{shinto-stub}} [[ja:武田神社]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = TakedaShrine.jpg | width = 250 | caption = Takeda Shrine | type = | dedication = | founded = [[1919]] | closed = | founder = | priest = | address = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, [[Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu]]
[[Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi]] | phone = | website = http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ }} {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[Japan]], and houses the deity of [[Takeda Shingen]]. ==See also== *[[Takeda clan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] {{shinto-stub}} [[ja:武田神社]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = TakedaShrine.jpg | width = 250 | caption = Takeda Shrine | type = | dedication = | founded = [[1919]] | closed = | founder = | priest = | address = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, [[Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu]]
[[Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi]] | phone = | website = http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ }} {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[Japan]], and houses the deity of [[Takeda Shingen]]. ==History== After the end of the [[Meiji Restoration]], a push began to honor the loyalists who had served during the time. Also, with the completion of the [[Russo-Japanese War]], a shrine was needed to honor the war dead. In [[1915]], the [[Emperor Taishō|Taishō Emperor]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in [[1919]]. The shrine's annual celebration is on [[April 12]], Shingen's [[death anniversary]]. ==See also== *[[Takeda clan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] {{shinto-stub}} [[ja:武田神社]]

武田神社 | image = KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | width = 250 | caption = Takeda Shrine | type = | dedication = [[Takeda Shingen]] | founded = 1919 | closed = | founder = | priest = | address = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, [[Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu]]
[[Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi]] | phone = | website = http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ }} [[Image:Takeda Shrine.JPG|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[Japan]], and houses the [[kami]] of [[Takeda Shingen]]. ==History== After the end of the [[Meiji Restoration]], a push began to honor the loyalists who had served during the time. Also, with the completion of the [[Russo-Japanese War]], a shrine was needed to honor the war dead. In 1915, the [[Emperor Taishō|Taishō Emperor]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[death anniversary]]. ==See also== *[[Takeda clan]] *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==External links== *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) {{coord|35|41|12.75|N|138|34|38.9|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] {{shinto-stub}} [[id:Kuil Takeda]] [[ja:武田神社]] [[pt:Takeda Jinja]] [[zh:武田神社]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | width = 250 | caption = Takeda Shrine | type = | dedication = [[Takeda Shingen]] | founded = 1919 | closed = | founder = | priest = | address = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, [[Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu]]
[[Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi]] | phone = 055-252-2609　 | website = http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ }} [[Image: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kōfu, Yamanashi|Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[Japan]], dedicated to the [[kami]] of [[Takeda Shingen]]. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[death anniversary]]. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Tsutsujigasaki Castle|Tsutsujigasaki]] fortified residence of [[Takeda Shingen]] was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]]. Following the visit of [[Emperor Meiji]] to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served during [[Boshin War]]. This dovetailed with the [[State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for the marital prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. In 1915, [[Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. ==Treasures== The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. It also houses a [[Kamakura period]] sword presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō family]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen. The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]]. ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) {{coord|35|41|12.75|N|138|34|38.9|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[id:Kuil Takeda]] [[ja:武田神社]] [[pt:Takeda Jinja]] [[zh:武田神社]]

In 1915, [[Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. ==Treasures== The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. It also houses a [[Kamakura period]] sword presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō family]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen. The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]]. ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) {{coord|35|41|12.75|N|138|34|38.9|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[fr:Takeda-jinja]] [[id:Kuil Takeda]] [[ja:武田神社]] [[pt:Takeda Jinja]] [[zh:武田神社]]

The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[death anniversary]]. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Tsutsujigasaki Castle|Tsutsujigasaki]] fortified residence of [[Takeda Shingen]] was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1915, [[Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late [[Kamakura period]]. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url= https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title= 山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01|language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35|41|12.75|N|138|34|38.9|E|region:JP-19_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Takeda Shingen]] | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in in the city of [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[Japan]], dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of [[Takeda Shingen]]. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[death anniversary]]. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Tsutsujigasaki Castle|Tsutsujigasaki]] fortified residence of [[Takeda Shingen]] was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of [[Emperor Meiji]] to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the [[Boshin War]]. This dovetailed with the [[State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. In 1915, [[Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late [[Kamakura period]]. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url= https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title= 山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01|language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35|41|12.75|N|138|34|38.9|E|region:JP-19_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Takeda Shingen]] | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[Japan]], dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of [[Takeda Shingen]]. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[death anniversary]]. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Tsutsujigasaki Castle|Tsutsujigasaki]] fortified residence of [[Takeda Shingen]] was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of [[Emperor Meiji]] to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the [[Boshin War]]. This dovetailed with the [[State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. In 1915, [[Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late [[Kamakura period]]. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]]

The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35|41|12.75|N|138|34|38.9|E|region:JP-19_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Takeda Shingen]] | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[Japan]], dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of [[Takeda Shingen]]. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[death anniversary]]. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Tsutsujigasaki Castle|Tsutsujigasaki]] fortified residence of [[Takeda Shingen]] was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of [[Emperor Meiji]] to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the [[Boshin War]]. This dovetailed with the [[State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. In 1915, [[Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late [[Kamakura period]]. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35|41|12.75|N|138|34|38.9|E|region:JP-19_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Takeda Shingen]] | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[Japan]], dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of [[Takeda Shingen]]. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[death anniversary]]. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Tsutsujigasaki Castle|Tsutsujigasaki]] fortified residence of [[Takeda Shingen]] was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of [[Emperor Meiji]] to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the [[Boshin War]]. This dovetailed with the [[State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. In 1915, [[Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late [[Kamakura period]]. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia30]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia30]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia23]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]]

The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late [[Kamakura period]]. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia05]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia05]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia23]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]]

It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35|41|12.75|N|138|34|38.9|E|region:JP-19_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Takeda Shingen]] | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[Japan]], dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of [[Takeda Shingen]]. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[death anniversary]]. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Tsutsujigasaki Castle|Tsutsujigasaki]] fortified residence of [[Takeda Shingen]] was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of [[Emperor Meiji]] to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the [[Boshin War]]. This dovetailed with the [[State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. In 1915, [[Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late [[Kamakura period]]. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[category:kk]][[category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[category:kk]][[category:Takeda clan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Ken-sha]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[category:mm]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]][[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[category:mm]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]]

The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[category:kk]][[category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[category:kk]][[category:Takeda clan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Ken-sha]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]][[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35|41|12.75|N|138|34|38.9|E|region:JP-19_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Takeda Shingen]] | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[Japan]], dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of [[Takeda Shingen]]. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[death anniversary]]. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Tsutsujigasaki Castle|Tsutsujigasaki]] fortified residence of [[Takeda Shingen]] was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of [[Emperor Meiji]] to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the [[Boshin War]]. This dovetailed with the [[State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. In 1915, [[Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late [[Kamakura period]]. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[category:kk]][[category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[category:kk]][[category:Takeda clan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Ken-sha]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]][[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:ファイル]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]]

The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[category:kk]][[category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[category:kk]][[category:Takeda clan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Ken-sha]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]][[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35|41|12.75|N|138|34|38.9|E|region:JP-19_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Takeda Shingen]] | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[Japan]], dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of [[Takeda Shingen]]. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[death anniversary]]. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Tsutsujigasaki Castle|Tsutsujigasaki]] fortified residence of [[Takeda Shingen]] was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of [[Emperor Meiji]] to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the [[Boshin War]]. This dovetailed with the [[State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. In 1915, [[Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late [[Kamakura period]]. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[category:kk]][[category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[category:kk]][[category:Takeda clan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Ken-sha]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]][[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]]

The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[category:kk]][[category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[category:kk]][[category:Takeda clan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Ken-sha]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]][[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35|41|12.75|N|138|34|38.9|E|region:JP-19_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Takeda Shingen]] | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[Japan]], dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of [[Takeda Shingen]]. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[death anniversary]]. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Tsutsujigasaki Castle|Tsutsujigasaki]] fortified residence of [[Takeda Shingen]] was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of [[Emperor Meiji]] to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the [[Boshin War]]. This dovetailed with the [[State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. In 1915, [[Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late [[Kamakura period]]. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[category:kk]][[category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[category:kk]][[category:Takeda clan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Ken-sha]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]][[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]]

The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35|41|12.75|N|138|34|38.9|E|region:JP-19_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Takeda Shingen]] | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[Japan]], dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of [[Takeda Shingen]]. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[death anniversary]]. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Tsutsujigasaki Castle|Tsutsujigasaki]] fortified residence of [[Takeda Shingen]] was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of [[Emperor Meiji]] to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the [[Boshin War]]. This dovetailed with the [[State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. In 1915, [[Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late [[Kamakura period]]. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]]

The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35|41|12.75|N|138|34|38.9|E|region:JP-19_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Takeda Shingen]] | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[Japan]], dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of [[Takeda Shingen]]. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[death anniversary]]. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Tsutsujigasaki Castle|Tsutsujigasaki]] fortified residence of [[Takeda Shingen]] was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of [[Emperor Meiji]] to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the [[Boshin War]]. This dovetailed with the [[State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. In 1915, [[Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late [[Kamakura period]]. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:転送]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]]

The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35|41|12.75|N|138|34|38.9|E|region:JP-19_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Takeda Shingen]] | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[Japan]], dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of [[Takeda Shingen]]. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[death anniversary]]. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the [[Sengoku period]], the [[Tsutsujigasaki Castle|Tsutsujigasaki]] fortified residence of [[Takeda Shingen]] was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of [[Emperor Meiji]] to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the [[Boshin War]]. This dovetailed with the [[State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the [[Russo-Japanese War]]. In 1915, [[Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late [[Kamakura period]]. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]][[en:Takeda Shrine]]
