{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q402368]] Wikidata name: Takeda Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35|41|12.75|N|138|34|38.9|E|region:JP-19_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]], dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]]. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[:en:Death anniversary|Death anniversary]]. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the [[:en:Tsutsujigasaki Castle|Tsutsujigasaki]] fortified residence of [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. This dovetailed with the [[:en:State Shintō|State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]]. In 1915, [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[:en:Lady Sanjō|Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[:en:Kanji|Kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan) | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] [[sn:Takeda Shrine]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]] {{Commons category|Takeda-jinja}} [[de:Takeda-Schrein]] [[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]] [[eu:Takeda santutegia]] [[fa:معبد تاکدا]] [[fr:Takeda-jinja]] [[id:Kuil Takeda]] [[ja:武田神社]] [[pt:Takeda Jinja]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]] [[zh:武田神社]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q402368]] Wikidata name: Takeda Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = File:KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{Coord|35.68666667|N|138.5775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Takeda Shingen|qid=Q276404}} | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kofu|qid=Q201932}} }} {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]], dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]]. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[:en:Death anniversary|Death anniversary]]. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the [[:en:Tsutsujigasaki Castle|Tsutsujigasaki]] fortified residence of [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. This dovetailed with the [[:en:State Shintō|State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]]. In 1915, [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[:en:Lady Sanjō|Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[:en:Kanji|Kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan) | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] [[sn:Takeda Shrine]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]] {{Commons category|Takeda-jinja}} [[de:Takeda-Schrein]] [[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]] [[eu:Takeda santutegia]] [[fa:معبد تاکدا]] [[fr:Takeda-jinja]] [[id:Kuil Takeda]] [[ja:武田神社]] [[pt:Takeda Jinja]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]] [[zh:武田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q402368}}

[[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]][[sn:commons:Category:Takeda-jinja]][[de:Takeda-Schrein]][[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[eu:Takeda santutegia]][[fa:معبد تاکدا]][[fr:Takeda-jinja]][[id:Kuil Takeda]][[ja:武田神社]][[pt:Takeda Jinja]][[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]][[zh:武田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q402368]] Wikidata name: Takeda Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = File:KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{Coord|35.68666667|N|138.5775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404}} | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kofu|WD=Q201932}} }} {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]], dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]]. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[:en:Death anniversary|Death anniversary]]. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the [[:en:Tsutsujigasaki Castle|Tsutsujigasaki]] fortified residence of [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. This dovetailed with the [[:en:State Shintō|State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]]. In 1915, [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[:en:Lady Sanjō|Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[:en:Kanji|Kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan) | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]] {{Commons category|Takeda-jinja}} [[de:Takeda-Schrein]] [[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]] [[eu:Takeda santutegia]] [[fa:معبد تاکدا]] [[fr:Takeda-jinja]] [[id:Kuil Takeda]] [[ja:武田神社]] [[pt:Takeda Jinja]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]] [[zh:武田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q402368}}

The sword is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan) | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]] {{Commons category|Takeda-jinja}} [[de:Takeda-Schrein]] [[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]] [[eu:Takeda santutegia]] [[fa:معبد تاکدا]] [[fr:Takeda-jinja]] [[id:Kuil Takeda]] [[ja:武田神社]] [[pt:Takeda Jinja]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]] [[zh:武田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q402368}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]][[sn:commons:Category:Takeda-jinja]][[de:Takeda-Schrein]][[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[eu:Takeda santutegia]][[fa:معبد تاکدا]][[fr:Takeda-jinja]][[id:Kuil Takeda]][[ja:武田神社]][[pt:Takeda Jinja]][[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]][[zh:武田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q402368]] Wikidata name: Takeda Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = File:KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{Coord|35.68666667|N|138.5775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404}} | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kofu|WD=Q201932}} }} {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]], dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]]. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[:en:Death anniversary|Death anniversary]]. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the [[:en:Tsutsujigasaki Castle|Tsutsujigasaki]] fortified residence of [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. This dovetailed with the [[:en:State Shintō|State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]]. In 1915, [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[:en:Lady Sanjō|Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[:en:Kanji|Kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan) | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]] {{Commons category|Takeda-jinja}} [[de:Takeda-Schrein]] [[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]] [[eu:Takeda santutegia]] [[fa:معبد تاکدا]] [[fr:Takeda-jinja]] [[id:Kuil Takeda]] [[ja:武田神社]] [[pt:Takeda Jinja]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]] [[zh:武田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q402368}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The sword is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan) | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]] {{Commons category|Takeda-jinja}} [[de:Takeda-Schrein]] [[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]] [[eu:Takeda santutegia]] [[fa:معبد تاکدا]] [[fr:Takeda-jinja]] [[id:Kuil Takeda]] [[ja:武田神社]] [[pt:Takeda Jinja]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]] [[zh:武田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q402368}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]][[sn:commons:Category:Takeda-jinja]][[de:Takeda-Schrein]][[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[eu:Takeda santutegia]][[fa:معبد تاکدا]][[fr:Takeda-jinja]][[id:Kuil Takeda]][[ja:武田神社]][[pt:Takeda Jinja]][[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]][[zh:武田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q402368]] Wikidata name: Takeda Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = File:KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{Coord|35.68666667|N|138.5775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404}} | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kofu|WD=Q201932}} }} {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]], dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]]. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[:en:Death anniversary|Death anniversary]]. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the [[:en:Tsutsujigasaki Castle|Tsutsujigasaki]] fortified residence of [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. This dovetailed with the [[:en:State Shintō|State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]]. In 1915, [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[:en:Lady Sanjō|Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[:en:Kanji|Kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan) | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]] {{Commons category|Takeda-jinja}} [[de:Takeda-Schrein]] [[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]] [[eu:Takeda santutegia]] [[fa:معبد تاکدا]] [[fr:Takeda-jinja]] [[id:Kuil Takeda]] [[ja:武田神社]] [[pt:Takeda Jinja]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]] [[zh:武田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q402368}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. This dovetailed with the [[:en:State Shintō|State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]]. In 1915, [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. 武田神社 | image = File:KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{Coord|35.68666667|N|138.5775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404}} | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kofu|WD=Q201932}} }} {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]], dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]]. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[:en:Death anniversary|Death anniversary]]. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the [[:en:Tsutsujigasaki Castle|Tsutsujigasaki]] fortified residence of [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. This dovetailed with the [[:en:State Shintō|State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]]. In 1915, [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[:en:Lady Sanjō|Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[:en:Kanji|Kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan) | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]] {{Commons category|Takeda-jinja}} [[de:Takeda-Schrein]] [[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]] [[eu:Takeda santutegia]] [[fa:معبد تاکدا]] [[fr:Takeda-jinja]] [[id:Kuil Takeda]] [[ja:武田神社]] [[pt:Takeda Jinja]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]] [[zh:武田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q402368}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's [[:en:Death anniversary|Death anniversary]]. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the [[:en:Tsutsujigasaki Castle|Tsutsujigasaki]] fortified residence of [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. This dovetailed with the [[:en:State Shintō|State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]]. In 1915, [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taishō]] commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when [[:en:Lady Sanjō|Lady Sanjō]] married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen]], although its half has only the ''[[:en:Kanji|Kanji]]'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan) | National Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *[[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]] {{Commons category|Takeda-jinja}} [[de:Takeda-Schrein]] [[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]] [[eu:Takeda santutegia]] [[fa:معبد تاکدا]] [[fr:Takeda-jinja]] [[id:Kuil Takeda]] [[ja:武田神社]] [[pt:Takeda Jinja]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]] [[zh:武田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q402368}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen}}, although its half has only the ''{{ill|Kanji|en|Kanji|WD=Q82772|lt=Kanji}}'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan) |en|Important Cultural Property (Japan) |WD=Q1188622|lt= National Important Cultural Property}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *{{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|lt=Takeda Shingen}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]] {{Commons category|Takeda-jinja}} [[de:Takeda-Schrein]] [[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]] [[eu:Takeda santutegia]] [[fa:معبد تاکدا]] [[fr:Takeda-jinja]] [[id:Kuil Takeda]] [[ja:武田神社]] [[pt:Takeda Jinja]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]] [[zh:武田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q402368}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]][[co:commons:Category:Takeda-jinja]][[de:Takeda-Schrein]][[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[eu:Takeda santutegia]][[fa:معبد تاکدا]][[fr:Takeda-jinja]][[id:Kuil Takeda]][[ja:武田神社]][[pt:Takeda Jinja]][[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]][[zh:武田神社]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]][[co:commons:Category:Takeda-jinja]][[de:Takeda-Schrein]][[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[eu:Takeda santutegia]][[fa:معبد تاکدا]][[fr:Takeda-jinja]][[id:Kuil Takeda]][[ja:武田神社]][[pt:Takeda Jinja]][[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]][[zh:武田神社]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]][[sn:commons:Category:Takeda-jinja]][[de:Takeda-Schrein]][[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[eu:Takeda santutegia]][[fa:معبد تاکدا]][[fr:Takeda-jinja]][[id:Kuil Takeda]][[ja:武田神社]][[pt:Takeda Jinja]][[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]][[zh:武田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q402368]] Wikidata name: Takeda Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = File:KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{Coord|35.68666667|N|138.5775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404}} | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kofu|WD=Q201932}} }} {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kōfu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|lt=Takeda Shingen}}. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's {{ill|Death anniversary|en|Death anniversary|WD=Q1202197|lt=Death anniversary}}. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, the {{ill|Tsutsujigasaki Castle|en|Tsutsujigasaki Castle|WD=Q11637200|lt=Tsutsujigasaki}} fortified residence of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|lt=Takeda Shingen}} was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji}} to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}. This dovetailed with the [[:en:State Shintō|State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}. In 1915, {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|WD=Q157052|lt=Emperor Taishō}} commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when {{ill|Lady Sanjō|en|Lady Sanjō|WD=Q422018|lt=Lady Sanjō}} married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen}}, although its half has only the ''{{ill|Kanji|en|Kanji|WD=Q82772|lt=Kanji}}'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan) |en|Important Cultural Property (Japan) |WD=Q1188622|lt= National Important Cultural Property}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *{{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|lt=Takeda Shingen}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]] {{Commons category|Takeda-jinja}} [[de:Takeda-Schrein]] [[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]] [[eu:Takeda santutegia]] [[fa:معبد تاکدا]] [[fr:Takeda-jinja]] [[id:Kuil Takeda]] [[ja:武田神社]] [[pt:Takeda Jinja]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]] [[zh:武田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q402368}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The sword is a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan) |en|Important Cultural Property (Japan) |WD=Q1188622|lt= National Important Cultural Property}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *{{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|lt=Takeda Shingen}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]] {{Commons category|Takeda-jinja}} [[de:Takeda-Schrein]] [[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]] [[eu:Takeda santutegia]] [[fa:معبد تاکدا]] [[fr:Takeda-jinja]] [[id:Kuil Takeda]] [[ja:武田神社]] [[pt:Takeda Jinja]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]] [[zh:武田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q402368}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

revision.text:
[[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]][[co:commons:Category:Takeda-jinja]][[de:Takeda-Schrein]][[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[eu:Takeda santutegia]][[fa:معبد تاکدا]][[fr:Takeda-jinja]][[id:Kuil Takeda]][[ja:武田神社]][[pt:Takeda Jinja]][[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]][[zh:武田神社]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]][[co:commons:Category:Takeda-jinja]][[de:Takeda-Schrein]][[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[eu:Takeda santutegia]][[fa:معبد تاکدا]][[fr:Takeda-jinja]][[id:Kuil Takeda]][[ja:武田神社]][[pt:Takeda Jinja]][[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]][[zh:武田神社]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]][[sn:commons:Category:Takeda-jinja]][[de:Takeda-Schrein]][[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[eu:Takeda santutegia]][[fa:معبد تاکدا]][[fr:Takeda-jinja]][[id:Kuil Takeda]][[ja:武田神社]][[pt:Takeda Jinja]][[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]][[zh:武田神社]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = File:KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{Coord|35.68666667|N|138.5775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404}} | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kofu|WD=Q201932}} }} {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kōfu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|lt=Takeda Shingen}}. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's {{ill|Death anniversary|en|Death anniversary|WD=Q1202197|lt=Death anniversary}}. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, the {{ill|Tsutsujigasaki Castle|en|Tsutsujigasaki Castle|WD=Q11637200|lt=Tsutsujigasaki}} fortified residence of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|lt=Takeda Shingen}} was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji}} to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}. This dovetailed with the [[:en:State Shintō|State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}. In 1915, {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|WD=Q157052|lt=Emperor Taishō}} commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when {{ill|Lady Sanjō|en|Lady Sanjō|WD=Q422018|lt=Lady Sanjō}} married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen}}, although its half has only the ''{{ill|Kanji|en|Kanji|WD=Q82772|lt=Kanji}}'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan) |en|Important Cultural Property (Japan) |WD=Q1188622|lt= National Important Cultural Property}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *{{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|lt=Takeda Shingen}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]] {{Commons category|Takeda-jinja}} [[de:Takeda-Schrein]] [[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]] [[eu:Takeda santutegia]] [[fa:معبد تاکدا]] [[fr:Takeda-jinja]] [[id:Kuil Takeda]] [[ja:武田神社]] [[pt:Takeda Jinja]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]] [[zh:武田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q402368}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

The sword is a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan) |en|Important Cultural Property (Japan) |WD=Q1188622|lt= National Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *{{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|lt=Takeda Shingen|1=Takeda Shingen}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]] {{Commons category|Takeda-jinja}} [[de:Takeda-Schrein]] [[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]] [[eu:Takeda santutegia]] [[fa:معبد تاکدا]] [[fr:Takeda-jinja]] [[id:Kuil Takeda]] [[ja:武田神社]] [[pt:Takeda Jinja]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]] [[zh:武田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q402368}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

[[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]][[co:commons:Category:Takeda-jinja]][[de:Takeda-Schrein]][[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[eu:Takeda santutegia]][[fa:معبد تاکدا]][[fr:Takeda-jinja]][[id:Kuil Takeda]][[ja:武田神社]][[pt:Takeda Jinja]][[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]][[zh:武田神社]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]][[co:commons:Category:Takeda-jinja]][[de:Takeda-Schrein]][[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[eu:Takeda santutegia]][[fa:معبد تاکدا]][[fr:Takeda-jinja]][[id:Kuil Takeda]][[ja:武田神社]][[pt:Takeda Jinja]][[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]][[zh:武田神社]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]][[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Takeda-jinja]][[de:Takeda-Schrein]][[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[eu:Takeda santutegia]][[fa:معبد تاکدا]][[fr:Takeda-jinja]][[id:Kuil Takeda]][[ja:武田神社]][[pt:Takeda Jinja]][[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]][[zh:武田神社]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = File:KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{Coord|35.68666667|N|138.5775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kofu|WD=Q201932|1=Kōfu}} }} {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kōfu|1=Kōfu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|lt=Takeda Shingen|1=Takeda Shingen}}. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's {{ill|Death anniversary|en|Death anniversary|WD=Q1202197|lt=Death anniversary|1=Death anniversary}}. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the {{ill|Tsutsujigasaki Castle|en|Tsutsujigasaki Castle|WD=Q11637200|lt=Tsutsujigasaki|1=Tsutsujigasaki Castle}} fortified residence of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|lt=Takeda Shingen|1=Takeda Shingen}} was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}} to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}. This dovetailed with the [[:en:State Shintō|State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War|1=Russo-Japanese War}}. In 1915, {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|WD=Q157052|lt=Emperor Taishō|1=Emperor Taishō}} commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when {{ill|Lady Sanjō|en|Lady Sanjō|WD=Q422018|lt=Lady Sanjō|1=Lady Sanjō}} married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen|1=Bizen Province}}, although its half has only the ''{{ill|Kanji|en|Kanji|WD=Q82772|lt=Kanji|1=Kanji}}'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan) |en|Important Cultural Property (Japan) |WD=Q1188622|lt= National Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *{{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|lt=Takeda Shingen|1=Takeda Shingen}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]] {{Commons category|Takeda-jinja}} [[de:Takeda-Schrein]] [[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]] [[eu:Takeda santutegia]] [[fa:معبد تاکدا]] [[fr:Takeda-jinja]] [[id:Kuil Takeda]] [[ja:武田神社]] [[pt:Takeda Jinja]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]] [[zh:武田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q402368}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen|1=Bizen Province}}, although its half has only the ''{{ill|Kanji|en|Kanji|WD=Q82772|lt=Kanji|1=Kanji}}'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan) |en|Important Cultural Property (Japan) |WD=Q1188622|lt= National Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *{{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|lt=Takeda Shingen|1=Takeda Shingen}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]] {{Commons category|Takeda-jinja}} [[de:Takeda-Schrein]] [[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]] [[eu:Takeda santutegia]] [[fa:معبد تاکدا]] [[fr:Takeda-jinja]] [[id:Kuil Takeda]] [[ja:武田神社]] [[pt:Takeda Jinja]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]] [[zh:武田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q402368}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]][[co:commons:Category:Takeda-jinja]][[de:Takeda-Schrein]][[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[eu:Takeda santutegia]][[fa:معبد تاکدا]][[fr:Takeda-jinja]][[id:Kuil Takeda]][[ja:武田神社]][[pt:Takeda Jinja]][[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]][[zh:武田神社]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]][[co:commons:Category:Takeda-jinja]][[de:Takeda-Schrein]][[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[eu:Takeda santutegia]][[fa:معبد تاکدا]][[fr:Takeda-jinja]][[id:Kuil Takeda]][[ja:武田神社]][[pt:Takeda Jinja]][[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]][[zh:武田神社]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]][[de:Takeda-Schrein]][[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]][[en:Takeda Shrine]][[eu:Takeda santutegia]][[fa:معبد تاکدا]][[fr:Takeda-jinja]][[id:Kuil Takeda]][[ja:武田神社]][[pt:Takeda Jinja]][[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]][[zh:武田神社]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Takeda Shrine
武田神社 | image = File:KofuTakedaJinja3.jpg | caption = Takeda Shrine | coordinates = {{Coord|35.68666667|N|138.5775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamanashi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} | founded_by = | established = 1919 | date_destroyed = | location = 2611 Kofuchū-machi, Kōfu-shi, Yamanashi-ken | website = {{URL|http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kofu|WD=Q201932|1=Kōfu}} }} {{nihongo|'''Takeda Shrine'''|武田神社|Takeda Jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kōfu|1=Kōfu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, dedicated to the ''[[kami]]'' of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|lt=Takeda Shingen|1=Takeda Shingen}}. The shrine's annual celebration is on April 12, Shingen's {{ill|Death anniversary|en|Death anniversary|WD=Q1202197|lt=Death anniversary|1=Death anniversary}}. ==History== Following the defeat of the [[Takeda clan]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the {{ill|Tsutsujigasaki Castle|en|Tsutsujigasaki Castle|WD=Q11637200|lt=Tsutsujigasaki|1=Tsutsujigasaki Castle}} fortified residence of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|lt=Takeda Shingen|1=Takeda Shingen}} was allowed to fall into ruins, and the center of Kōfu shifted south to surround [[Maizuru Castle Park|Kōfu Castle]], the center of administrative power under the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the Tsutsujigasaki ruins came under government protection for their historic value and were eventually made a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Monument of Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138294 |title=武田氏館跡 |website=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 September 2018}} Following the visit of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}} to Yamanashi Prefecture in 1880, a local movement developed for a shrine to honor the loyalists who had served in the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}. This dovetailed with the [[:en:State Shintō|State Shintō]] projects to erect shrines dedicated to historic figures noted for their martial prowess and with the need for a shrine to honor the war dead of the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War|1=Russo-Japanese War}}. In 1915, {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|WD=Q157052|lt=Emperor Taishō|1=Emperor Taishō}} commissioned the shrine, which was completed in 1919. The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] before World War II. The shrine grounds contain a museum, in which numerous artifacts pertaining to Takeda Shingen, including armor, weapons, battle standards, and personal effects, are displayed. ==Cultural properties== ===National Important Cultural Property=== The Takeda Shrine owns a ''[[tachi]]'' [[Japanese sword]] presented to Yamanashi Prefecture by [[Sanjō Sanetomi]] to commemorate the visit by Emperor Meiji in 1880. The sword had passed into the [[Sanjō clan]] as part of the bridal gifts from the Takeda clan when {{ill|Lady Sanjō|en|Lady Sanjō|WD=Q422018|lt=Lady Sanjō|1=Lady Sanjō}} married Takeda Shingen, and dates from the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}. It has a length of 64.5 cm and a width of 2.9 cm. The sword is attributed to the noted swordsmith Yoshioka Ichimonji from {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen|1=Bizen Province}}, although its half has only the ''{{ill|Kanji|en|Kanji|WD=Q82772|lt=Kanji|1=Kanji}}'' for "ichi" graved upon it. The sword is a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan) |en|Important Cultural Property (Japan) |WD=Q1188622|lt= National Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）工芸品01 |language=Japanese |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate= |archive-date=2013-06-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130602212113/http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gakujutu/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kougeihin001.html#k0002ll |url-status=dead }} ==See also== *{{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|lt=Takeda Shingen|1=Takeda Shingen}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commonscat|Takeda-jinja}} *[http://www.takedajinja.or.jp/ Official site] (Japanese) *[http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php Kōfu city official sightseeing guide] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211109150525/http://en.kofushingen.jp/tourism/t01.php |date=2021-11-09 }} *[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/foreign/english/spot/006.html Yamanashi Prefecture official sightseeing guide] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[file: KofuTakedaJinja1.jpg|thumb|right|Main gate to Takeda Shrine]] [[en:Takeda Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تاكيدا]] {{Commons category|Takeda-jinja}} [[de:Takeda-Schrein]] [[el:Ιερό Τακέντα]] [[eu:Takeda santutegia]] [[fa:معبد تاکدا]] [[fr:Takeda-jinja]] [[id:Kuil Takeda]] [[ja:武田神社]] [[pt:Takeda Jinja]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าทาเกดะ]] [[zh:武田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q402368}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
